situation: audit-2026-07-06-021 derived_fixes last pending item. relay_bridge.py:107 returned True when Origin header absent, letting attackers skip CSWSH allowlist by omitting the header. Token is primary defense but defense-in-depth requires both gates.
action: Added _is_browser_client() heuristic (Sec-WebSocket-Key or Mozilla UA) and tightened _origin_ok() to reject browser clients that omit Origin while continuing to allow non-browser clients (tunnel_client.py, native apps) without Origin. Logged browser_missing_origin for fail2ban.
outcome_evidence: commit 0d97179 on auto/opt-relay-bridge-1783342228 pushed to origin; git log shows opt(relay-bridge) commit atop d6f650b main base. Code review URL emitted by push remote.
next_suggestion: All audit-2026-07-06-021 derived_fixes now merged/rejected — cycle-complete. Next round triggers new full deep-audit on an un-audited project (candidates not yet in history: wechat-main, moni-master).
DRIFT ALERT (carried): H-001 worktree opt-ws-auth-1783338961 has fix commit 48555aa but not merged to main >12h — needs user decision before next audit starts.

audit_findings.finding: Origin validation bypassed when Origin header absent — attacker can omit header to skip ALLOWED_ORIGINS check entirely
audit_findings.remediation: Detect browser clients via Sec-WebSocket-Key / Mozilla UA; require Origin only for browsers, continue allowing non-browser clients without Origin (token gate still applies)
